# The Woolpack Inn

## Business Summary
The Woolpack is a historic 300-year-old country pub nestled in the Slad Valley, Stroud, Gloucestershire (GL6 7QA). Established in 1742, it is one of the most celebrated rural pubs in the Cotswolds, renowned for its timeless charm, fireside atmosphere, seasonal food, and community spirit.

## Location
The Woolpack Inn, Slad Road, Slad, Stroud, Gloucestershire, GL6 7QA
Phone: 01452 234290
Email: woolpackslad@gmail.com

## Opening Hours
- Bar & Snug: Sun–Mon 12pm–10pm | Tue–Thu 12pm–11pm | Fri–Sat 12pm–12am
- Dining Room Lunch: Tue–Sat 12pm–2:30pm | Sun 12pm–3:30pm
- Dining Room Dinner: Tue–Sat 6pm–9pm | Sun 6pm–8:30pm

## What We Offer
- Real ales, craft beers and fine ciders from local Cotswold breweries
- Seasonal bar kitchen snacks (toasties, olives, soup, daily specials)
- Full dining room with weekly-changing seasonal menus
- Fireside tavern, dining room, snug and vine-covered terraces
- Book via SevenRooms: https://www.sevenrooms.com/landing/thewoolpackinn

## Food Philosophy
Head Chef Adam Glover leads a kitchen that is strictly seasonal and ingredient-led. Menus change weekly, driven by produce from our own two-acre kitchen gardens at Lypiatt Park — grown using organic, biodynamic and no-dig practices. Vegetables, herbs and edible flowers are delivered twice weekly from our head grower Tomer Shaltiel.

## Team
- Dan Chadwick — Owner (purchased freehold 1999)
- Adam Glover — Head Chef (joined 2013)
- Nige — Senior Sous Chef (10+ years, famous for Pig's Head & Potato Pie)
- Elliot Lyndley — Assistant General Manager
- Hero — Restaurant Manager

## Heritage
The Woolpack was a regular haunt of Laurie Lee, author of the beloved Cotswold memoir *Cider with Rosie*. The pub holds a collection of Laurie Lee beer bottles and a cabinet of his written works. Lee is buried in the nearby Slad Churchyard.

## Awards & Recognition
- National Pub & Bar Awards
- Estrella Damm recognition
- Trenchermans Guide listed
- Featured by: Matthew Fort (Great British Menu), Tom Parker Bowles (Daily Mail), Giles Coren (The Times), Grace Dent (The Guardian), The Observer

## Sister Venue
Juliet Restaurant — http://www.julietrestaurant.co.uk/
Shared kitchen garden at Lypiatt Park supplies both venues.

## Nearby Stays
- The Lodge: https://www.staycotswold.com/property/the-lodge/
- The Gatehouse: https://www.staycotswold.com/property/the-gatehouse/
